commit.message:
v0.1.61: datum fix, spawn fix, remove OSM mesh overlays

- Add egm96_undulation() to elevation.rs: regional polynomial fits for
  AU/EU/NA/Indian Ocean; global zonal-harmonic fallback (±3m AU, ±8m global)

- Fix terrain datum mismatch: SRTM sources return EGM96 orthometric heights
  but GPS.alt is WGS-84 ellipsoidal. Apply N = egm96_undulation(lat,lon)
  in Pass 1 before computing surface_voxel_y so both are in the same datum.
  Same correction applied in road-carving and waterway fallback queries.

- Fix spawn: origin moved from Story Bridge (over water, unreliable SRTM)
  to Kangaroo Point south bank (solid land). origin_gps.alt now set from
  blocking SRTM query + EGM96 undulation → player spawns at terrain surface.

- Improve water bank elevation: instead of min in-water column Y (noisy),
  use min of adjacent non-water columns within 6-voxel radius as water level.
  Falls back to min in-water Y if no land neighbours in chunk.

- Remove OSM mesh overlay system for roads/railways/waterways/parks/water
  polygons (both loading and game phases). These flat quads appeared at
  wrong elevations because query_nonblocking returned default before tiles
  were cached. Voxel materials (ASPHALT/WATER/GRASS/CONCRETE) are now the
  sole representation of all OSM features.

- Keep world objects (buildings, streetlights) — they are proper 3D objects
  with EGM96-corrected Y placement.

- Remove road labels HUD overlay (depended on removed road_segments data).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[msbuild] Don't pass symbol file to strip when _ExportSymbolsExplicitly=false. Fixes #24582. (#24800)

When _ExportSymbolsExplicitly is false, the linker uses -u flags instead of
-exported_symbols_list, so mtouch-symbols.list is not transferred to the
remote Mac. Don't pass it to strip either, since the file won't exist.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/macios/issues/24582.

_update_conversation_id assumed chat_options had attribute access, but
ChatOptions is a TypedDict (dict). When a dict was passed, setting

commit.message:
Fix: remove corrupt PNG snapshots and stabilize mobile navigation (#107)

All E2E tests were failing due to two independent issues: corrupt
baseline PNG snapshots and a race condition in mobile navigation tests.

### Corrupt PNG snapshots (45 of 78 files)

Chunk-level PNG validation revealed widespread corruption — CRC
mismatches in IDAT chunks, trailing bytes after IEND, truncated chunks.
The `file` command masks this since it only checks magic bytes, but
Playwright's parser is strict. Root cause is likely the CI screenshot
merge workflow producing partial overwrites when new screenshots are
shorter than old ones.

Deleted all 45 corrupt files. CI runs with `--update-snapshots` and will
regenerate them.

### Mobile navigation test timing

`mobile-navigation.spec.ts` interacted with the dropdown immediately
after `page.goto()` without waiting for hydration. React re-rendering
during hydration disrupts the daisyUI CSS `:focus-within` dropdown
state.

Added page content waits before dropdown interaction, matching the
pattern already used in passing tests:

```typescript
// Before interacting with dropdown, wait for page hydration
await expect(page.locator("h1").filter({ hasText: "Notes" })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 })
await page.getByRole("button", { name: "Open user menu" }).click()
```

commit.message:
feat(svg-core)!: Migrate to Observable pattern with class-based architecture (#730)

## Type of Change

- [x] `feat` — New feature (major refactoring with breaking changes)

## Related Issue

No GitHub issue (breaking change refactoring)

## Context

The previous `getSvg()` / `fetchSvg()` function-based API lacked
reactivity and made state management cumbersome. The new Observer
pattern-based architecture provides a class-centric, reactive API that
is more maintainable, testable, and aligned with modern JavaScript
practices. This refactoring improves the developer experience and sets a
solid foundation for framework integration.

## Changes

- Introduced `SvgStore` class as the main entry point (replaces
`getSvg()`)
- Implemented Observer pattern for reactive state management via
`subscribe()`
- Created abstraction layer with interface definitions (`.d.ts` files)
for all major components
- Refactored pipeline into focused, single-responsibility classes:
  - `SvgFetcher` — HTTP fetching with deduplication
  - `SvgSourceResolver` — URL vs. data URI detection
  - `SvgInlineDecoder` — Base64 decoding for data URIs
  - `SvgSanitizer` — DOMPurify integration
  - `SvgMerger` — Attribute and content merging
  - `SvgValidator` — SVG validation
  - `Container` — Dependency injection container
  - `AbstractObserver` — Observable state management
- Removed legacy function exports (`getSvg`, `fetchSvg`,
`mergeSvgContent`, etc.)
- Updated README with quick start, full API reference, and migration
guide for v2 → v3
- Full test coverage for new architecture (SvgStore, all components,
edge cases)

## Checklist

- [x] My branch is named `feature/…` or `bugfix/…`
(feature/migrate-to-observable-patern)
- [x] Commits follow Conventional Commits with correct scope
(`svg-core`)
- [x] Tests are in `tests/` and passing (`npm run test` passes)
- [x] `npm run lint` passes
- [x] Documentation updated (README with migration guide)
- [x] No TypeScript errors
- [x] All existing functionality preserved (different API surface)

## Additional Notes

BREAKING CHANGE: The library API has been completely redesigned. The
old function-based API (`getSvg()`, `fetchSvg()`) is replaced with a
class-based Observable pattern. See the [Migration Guide in
README](packages/svg-core/README.md#-migration-from-v2-to-v3) for
detailed upgrade instructions.

**Why this breaking change?**
The previous implementation wasn't meeting evolving needs around
reactivity and state management. A class-based approach with the
Observer pattern provides:
- ✅ Reactive state updates via `subscribe()`
- ✅ Better testability with dependency injection
- ✅ Cleaner framework integration (React 19 Suspense, Vue reactivity)
- ✅ Future-proof architecture for advanced features

**Impact:**
- 🔴 **Consumers:** Must update all `getSvg()` / `fetchSvg()` calls to
use `new SvgStore()` + `subscribe()` pattern
- 🟢 **svg-react:** Will be updated in follow-up PR to use the new
Observable API
- 🟢 **Storybook:** Will be updated in follow-up PR

**Testing:**
- ✅ All new components have comprehensive test coverage (800+ lines of
new tests)
- ✅ Edge cases covered: network errors, malformed SVG, concurrent
requests
- ✅ Integration tests verify the full pipeline
- ✅ Type safety maintained with TypeScript strict mode
